channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
item.description: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.
item.content:encoded:
Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.

(Image credit: Vincent Thian/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Iran rejects U.S. peace plan. And, jury finds Meta, Google to blame in addiction trial
item.description: Iran rejects a U.S. proposal to end the war and counters with a different peace plan. And, a jury finds Meta and Google negligent in a trial over social media's harms.
item.content:encoded:
Iran rejects a U.S. proposal to end the war and counters with a different peace plan. And, a jury finds Meta and Google negligent in a trial over social media's harms.

(Image credit: Abbas Fakih)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Israel and Iran ramp up attacks as Trump insists Tehran wants a deal
item.description: The war in the Middle East ramped up on Thursday as Israel launched a wave of strikes targeting Iranian infrastructure, and Iran fired rounds of missiles at central Israel.
item.content:encoded:
The war in the Middle East ramped up on Thursday as Israel launched a wave of strikes targeting Iranian infrastructure, and Iran fired rounds of missiles at central Israel.

(Image credit: Amir Levy)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Trump's attacks on offshore wind could hurt infrastructure spending across the economy
item.description: President Trump has tried to kill offshore wind's future in the U.S. But industry analysts say the attacks could hurt business confidence across the U.S. economy.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has tried to kill offshore wind's future in the U.S. But industry analysts say the attacks could hurt business confidence across the U.S. economy.

(Image credit: DON EMMERT/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley

item.title: It's Equal Pay Day. Women have lost ground for the second year in a row
item.description: The annual observance marks how far into the new year women must work to make what men earned in the previous year. This year, it's March 26, a day later than it was in 2025.
item.content:encoded:
The annual observance marks how far into the new year women must work to make what men earned in the previous year. This year, it's March 26, a day later than it was in 2025.

(Image credit: simplehappyart)
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Decades ago, a Maryland sailor burned his winter socks. Now it's a spring tradition
item.description: In Annapolis, Md., people gather each year to usher in the warmer weather by burning their socks. The springtime tradition is the unofficial start of the Chesapeake Bay sailing season.
item.content:encoded:
In Annapolis, Md., people gather each year to usher in the warmer weather by burning their socks. The springtime tradition is the unofficial start of the Chesapeake Bay sailing season.

(Image credit: Tyrone Turner)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: A $50 billion fund to help rural hospitals could actually lead to closures and cuts
item.description: States are rolling out plans for their share of a $50 billion fund established by Congress to improve rural health care. In some states, the money may provoke rural hospitals to cut services.
item.content:encoded:
States are rolling out plans for their share of a $50 billion fund established by Congress to improve rural health care. In some states, the money may provoke rural hospitals to cut services.

(Image credit: Aaron Bolton)
item.dc:creator: Aaron Bolton

item.title: Hoping to unseat Collins, Maine Democrats battle it out in an expensive U.S. Senate primary
item.description: As June's primary election nears, Democratic Gov. Janet Mills and combat veteran Graham Platner are effectively engaged in a proxy battle between factions in their own party.
item.content:encoded:
As June's primary election nears, Democratic Gov. Janet Mills and combat veteran Graham Platner are effectively engaged in a proxy battle between factions in their own party.

(Image credit: Robert F. Bukaty/AP; Kevin Dietsch/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Steve Mistler

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Iran rejects U.S. peace proposal and lays out its own conditions, the Army's 82nd Airborne Division readies to deploy to Iran, jury finds Meta and Google liable in social media addiction trial.
item.content:encoded: Iran rejects U.S. peace proposal and lays out its own conditions, the Army's 82nd Airborne Division readies to deploy to Iran, jury finds Meta and Google liable in social media addiction trial.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Melania Trump shares the spotlight with a robot at an education and technology event
item.description: The robot accompanied the first lady to the White House East Room for the final day of a summit she had convened with counterparts from around the world through her Fostering the Future Together global initiative.
item.content:encoded:
The robot accompanied the first lady to the White House East Room for the final day of a summit she had convened with counterparts from around the world through her Fostering the Future Together global initiative.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
